**Minecraft Version:** 26.1.2

**NeoForge Version:** 26.1.2.78

**Logs:** N/A

**Steps to Reproduce:**
 1. Try using `RegisterGameRuleEntryFactoryEvent` to register a client game rule GUI entry.
 2. Look for implementations of `GameRuleEntryFactoryManager#appendGameRuleEntry`.
 3. Look at the GitHub repository to find a `.patch` file.
 4. Nothing is found.

**Description of issue:**
`GameRuleEntryFactoryManager#appendGameRuleEntry` is not implemented within a relevant method. This makes all `RegisterGameRuleEntryFactoryEvent`s not count.

title: NeoForge does not detect double registration across multiple deferred registries
author: jcotton42
body:
**Minecraft Version:** 1.21.1

**NeoForge Version:** 21.1.235

**Logs:** https://github.com/jcotton42/double-registration-sync-error-repro/tree/main/logs

**Example code:** https://github.com/jcotton42/double-registration-sync-error-repro

**Steps to Reproduce:**
1. Create two DeferredRegisters to the same registry, and register an item to each with the same ID
https://github.com/jcotton42/double-registration-sync-error-repro/blob/292291562fdff28f08fc535659d7d69f02a9119f/src/main/java/com/example/examplemod/DoubleRegistrationError.java#L18-L23
2. Force enumeration of the registry at runtime
https://github.com/jcotton42/double-registration-sync-error-repro/blob/292291562fdff28f08fc535659d7d69f02a9119f/src/main/java/com/example/examplemod/DoubleRegistrationErrorClient.java#L34-L36
3. Join a dedicated server from the client (the internal server used for singleplayer does not trip this bug) and force the above enumeration (the example repo does this when you press <kbd>C</kbd>)

**Description of issue:**
NeoForge appears to not catch duplicated registrations when those registrations are spread across multiple DeferredRegisters. During the registry sync when connecting to a multiplayer server, this results in a null hole in the registry where one of duplicates used to be. Anything that looks up the registry then risks a NullPointerException crash since the iterator invokes `Holder::value()` on every entry.

https://github.com/jcotton42/double-registration-sync-error-repro/blob/292291562fdff28f08fc535659d7d69f02a9119f/logs/client-latest.log#L1742-L1805

The crash does not occur in singleplayer, unless the player joined a multiplayer server and left without crashing.

title: Common network protocol setup skipped due to race condition
author: Su5eD
body:
**Minecraft Version:** 26.1.2
**NeoForge Version:** 26.1.2.76

Other versions are most likely affected as well.

## The problem

There is race condition in the network code due to which client channel registration is only received by the server after configuration tasks have been registered (see `ConfigurationInitialization::configureModdedClient`).

This results in the common version and register config tasks being skipped, and therefore the peers not exchanging information on sendable channels through the common `c` protocol.

In my case this happens with a mod running on Forgified Fabric API, but it can be easily reproduced by trying to join a Fabric server using a NeoForge client (see steps to reproduce below).

In a diagram, our flow looks like this:
```
Server                                           Client
======                                           ======
  |                                                 |
  | -------- REGISTER ----------------------------> |
  | -------- QUERY -------------------------------> |
  | -------- PING --------------------------------> |
  |                                                 |
  |                              (REGISTER received,|
  |                               but no response)  |
  |                                                 |
  | <------------------------------- QUERY -------- |
  | <-------------------------------- PONG -------- |
  | -------- PAYLOAD -----------------------------> |  (response to QUERY)
  | -------- REGISTER ----------------------------> |  (response to QUERY)
  |                                                 |
  | (Handles PONG and                               |
  |  schedules registration tasks)                  |  <-- (A) should only happen after (B) handled by server
  |                                                 |
  | <---------------------------- REGISTER -------- |  <-- (B) too late now, must happen before (A)
  | (Processes client registration                  |
  |  after deadline)                                |
  |                                                 |
```

In comparison, Fabric starts its configurations phase ONLY when it receives a registration payload from the client, which guarantees it has information on all payload types the client understands. Their flow looks as follows:
```
Server                                           Client
======                                           ======
  |                                                 |
  | -------- REGISTER ----------------------------> |
  | -------- PING --------------------------------> |
  | <---------------------------- REGISTER -------- |  <-- server detects modded connection
  |                                                 |
  | (Begins configuration safely)                   |
  |                                                 |
  | <-------------------------------- PONG -------- |
  | (PONG discarded — this is a modded connection)  |
  |                                                 |
```

## Steps to Reproduce

 1. Prepare a Fabric dev environment (you can use the official [example mod](https://github.com/FabricMC/fabric-example-mod/tree/26.1.2))
 2. Prepare a NeoForge client
 3. Start the Fabric server
 4. On the server, place a breakpoint into `net.fabricmc.fabric.impl.networking.CommonPacketsImpl#init` on the line that says `listener.addTask(new CommonVersionConfigurationTask(addon));` (~77). The point of this is that the code runs only if the common packets have been registered by the NeoForge client in time.
 6. Connect to the server from the NeoForge client
 7. Wait for the breakpoint to be triggered
 8. If it does NOT trigger before the neoforge client enters the world, you have successfully reproduced the issue
> [!WARNING]
> This is a data race issue, and will not occur constantly. If it does not work for you immediately, keep retrying or switching environments.

## Expected result

The breakpoint is triggered - this means the server is aware the client understands the common protocol.

## Actual result

The breakpoint MIGHT NOT be triggered - this happens due to the race condition described above.

## Proposed solution

A simple solution I tried and fixed the issue with is responding to the registration payload in `ClientConfigurationPacketListenerImpl::handleCustomPayload` with our builtin channels.

This is a non-breaking change with no side effects and due to the sending order it guarantees the server has information on the channels before PONG arrives.

```patch
  @Override
  public void handleCustomPayload(net.minecraft.network.protocol.common.ClientboundCustomPayloadPacket packet) {
    // ...
+   if (packet.payload() instanceof net.neoforged.neoforge.network.payload.MinecraftRegisterPayload registerPayload) {
+      net.neoforged.neoforge.client.network.registration.ClientNetworkRegistry.sendInitialListeningChannels(this);
+      return;
+   }
    // ...
  }
```

## Relevant sources

- https://github.com/neoforged/NeoForge/pull/277
- https://github.com/neoforged/NeoForge/pull/1483
- `net.minecraft.client.multiplayer.ClientConfigurationPacketListenerImpl`
- `net.minecraft.server.network.ServerConfigurationPacketListenerImpl`

title: `IRecipeOutputExtension` makes it impossible to extend `RecipeOutput` in a multi-loader project
author: Fuzss
body:
<!-- Thank you for reporting! -->

<!-- For support with crashes and issues, please make a post in #user-support channel in NeoForge Discord instead: -->
<!-- https://discord.gg/UvedJ9m -->

**Minecraft Version:** 26.2

**NeoForge Version:** 26.2.0.6-beta

**Description of issue:**
[This](https://github.com/neoforged/NeoForge/blob/4a632391aa4df8ef9b8b0521ab84da5a90bbfc94/src/main/java/net/neoforged/neoforge/common/extensions/IRecipeOutputExtension.java#L27) abstract method added to `RecipeOutput` by NeoForge makes it impossible to implement the `RecipeOutput` interface in a common multi-loader project.

The issue is present as far back as at least 1.21.1.

**Example:**
This class is placed in common and compiles fine on Fabric (Minecraft 1.21.1).
```java
public record TransformingRecipeOutput(RecipeOutput recipeOutput,
                                       UnaryOperator<Recipe<?>> operator) implements RecipeOutput {

    public static RecipeOutput transformed(RecipeOutput recipeOutput, UnaryOperator<Recipe<?>> operator) {
        return new TransformingRecipeOutput(recipeOutput, operator);
    }

    @Override
    public void accept(ResourceLocation resourceLocation, Recipe<?> recipe, @Nullable AdvancementHolder advancement) {
        this.recipeOutput.accept(resourceLocation, this.operator().apply(recipe), advancement);
    }

    @Override
    public Advancement.Builder advancement() {
        return this.recipeOutput.advancement();
    }
}
```

On NeoForge, compilation fails due to this error.
```
./puzzles-lib/1.21.1/Common/src/main/java/fuzs/puzzleslib/api/data/v2/recipes/TransformingRecipeOutput.java:15: Fehler: TransformingRecipeOutput ist nicht abstrakt und setzt die abstrakte Methode accept(ResourceLocation,Recipe<?>,AdvancementHolder,ICondition...) in IRecipeOutputExtension nicht außer Kraft
public record TransformingRecipeOutput(RecipeOutput recipeOutput,
       ^
```

title: `@Overwrite` mixin visibility ignores access transformer changes
author: SuperMartijn642

title: Vanilla client disconnects when receiving an item with a NeoForge added attribute
author: pupnewfster
body:
**Minecraft Version:** 26.2

**NeoForge Version:** 26.2.0.7-beta

**Logs:** https://gist.github.com/pupnewfster/9113765f3e00e95f19a13be056792ff3

**Steps to Reproduce:**
 1. Start a Neo Server
 2. Start a vanilla client
 3. Connect to the Neo server with the vanilla client
 4. Give yourself op or adjust the following command to be given to the player from the console
 5. Run `/give @s minecraft:stick[minecraft:attribute_modifiers=[{id:"neo:test",type:"neoforge:creative_flight",slot:"any",operation:"add_value",amount:1}]]` (The command will be red on the client, but hit enter anyway)
 6. Client will disconnect with the reason `Internal Exception: io.netty.handler.codec.DecoderException: Failed to decode packet 'clientbound/minecraft:set_entity_data'`

**Description of issue:**
I discovered this while testing vanilla compat for #3192, and when a vanilla client is given an item with a neoforge added attribute, they crash. Presumably because `VanillaConnectionNetworkFilter` is filtering `ClientboundUpdateAttributesPacket` but not packets that contain stacks that might include those attributes.

title: DoubleValue config screen prevents entering decimal separator after deleting it
author: mossssfet
body:
<!-- Thank you for reporting! -->

<!-- For support with crashes and issues, please make a post in #user-support channel in NeoForge Discord instead: -->
<!-- https://discord.gg/UvedJ9m -->

**Minecraft Version:** 26.2

**NeoForge Version:** 26.2.0.6-beta

**Logs:** N/A

**Steps to Reproduce:**
1. Create a `ModConfigSpec.DoubleValue` config entry, for example:

```java
public static final ModConfigSpec.DoubleValue EXAMPLE = BUILDER
        .comment("xyzw")
        .defineInRange("example", 20.0, 0.0, Double.MAX_VALUE);
```

2. Launch the game and open the config screen.
3. Locate the `DoubleValue` entry and edit its value.
4. Remove the decimal point from the existing value (for example, change `20.0` to `20`).
5. Attempt to type `.` again to enter a decimal value.

**Expected Behavior**: The decimal point should be accepted, allowing values such as `20.5` to be entered.
**Actual Behavior**: After the decimal point has been removed, pressing `.` has no effect and the field no longer accepts decimal separators. The value can only be edited as an integer unless the screen is reopened or the value is reset.

**Description of issue:**
`ModConfigSpec.DoubleValue` entries appear to stop accepting decimal separators once the existing decimal point has been deleted. This makes it impossible to enter a new fractional value during the same editing session.

title: Remove NeoForge's `nametag_distance` attribute in favor of vanilla's `name_tag_distance`
author: sciwhiz12
body:
NeoForge adds the `neoforge:nametag_distance` attribute to control the distance at which name tags (above entity/player heads) are visible to the user. 

However in 26.2, Minecraft added the `minecraft:name_tag_distance` which effectively replaces our attribute. It seems this was noticed in porting, as almost nothing references the NeoForge-added attribute.

The one remaining place it is mentioned is in `LivingEntity#shouldShowName`, where it takes the place of a fixed max-distance check used for discreet entities (e.g., crouching players):

https://github.com/neoforged/NeoForge/blob/4a632391aa4df8ef9b8b0521ab84da5a90bbfc94/patches/net/minecraft/client/renderer/entity/LivingEntityRenderer.java.patch#L19-L27

Additionally, `EntityRenderer#extractNameTags` is bugged because it was not properly fixed to use the MC attribute value, probably as a consequence of the NeoForge-added attribute being ripped out. It should be using the `nameTagDistance` parameter, which is set to the value of the attribute:

https://github.com/neoforged/NeoForge/blob/4a632391aa4df8ef9b8b0521ab84da5a90bbfc94/patches/net/minecraft/client/renderer/entity/EntityRenderer.java.patch#L36-L48
